entities.name: WEST AFRICAN SAVANNA
entities.description: The region in Africa characterized by savanna vegetation where agricultural changes are studied

entities.name: POPULATION GROWTH
entities.description: The increase in human population acting as a driver for changes in farming systems

entities.name: LOW RAINFALL
entities.description: Continuing lack of sufficient precipitation affecting agricultural productivity

entities.name: VILLAGE FARMING SYSTEMS
entities.description: The local agricultural practices and structures in rural areas

entities.name: AGRO-ECOLOGICAL ZONES
entities.description: The three main ecological divisions used to categorize farming in Burkina Faso

entities.name: BURKINA FASO
entities.description: The West African country where the farming systems were described

entities.name: TOPOSEQUENTIAL LANDUSE
entities.description: The pattern of land use based on the topographical sequence of the landscape

entities.name: CROPPING PATTERNS
entities.description: The specific arrangements and sequences of crops grown in the farming systems

entities.name: MODEL
entities.description: A tool used to describe long-term ecological degradation based on landuse and cropping patterns

entities.name: ECOLOGICAL DEGRADATION
entities.description: The long-term decline in the quality of the environment due to farming practices

entities.name: FALLOW LAND
entities.description: Land left uncultivated to restore its fertility

entities.name: OVER-CROPPING
entities.description: The practice of cropping land too intensively without adequate fallow periods

entities.name: LAND TENURE SYSTEMS
entities.description: The rules and customs governing the ownership and use of land

entities.name: LAND DEGRADATION
entities.description: The current environmental problem characterized by the decline of land productivity in the West African savanna

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL RESEARCH
entities.description: The scientific study aimed at finding solutions for land degradation and improving farming
